title: Grief Stages Calculator: Family Loss 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Stages of Grief in Family Loss
description: Free grief calculator: understand the 5 stages after family loss. See typical duration and warning signs based on loss type.
intro: Grief and loss are universal experiences, yet each person's journey is unique. Understanding the emotional stages of grief helps families process loss and recognize when additional support is needed. Whether facing loss through long-term illness, sudden loss, or loss during childhood, this free tool explains the typical stages, duration, and warning signs using the evidence-based Kübler-Ross model.
keyTakeaway: Kübler-Ross model: denial, anger, bargaining, depression, acceptance. Typical duration 6-12 months. Results vary by loss type.
useCases: Students studying grief psychology or counseling
Mental health professionals creating treatment plans
Family members understanding their grief journey
Therapists applying the Kübler-Ross model in sessions
Anyone seeking to understand typical grief timelines and expectations
explanation:
Grief after family loss is a natural emotional response. While often described in stages, grief is not strictly linear—people experience phases in different orders, may skip stages, or revisit them multiple times.

## How This Calculator Works

This tool applies the Kübler-Ross grief model based on your input, showing the typical stages, duration, and warning signs. The five classic stages are denial, anger, bargaining, depression, and acceptance.

## Understanding Grief Duration

Most people experience acute grief for 6-12 months. However, grief adjustment continues for years, with intensity gradually decreasing. Duration varies based on loss type, relationship to the deceased, and available support.

## Important Notes

This is an orientative reference based on established psychological frameworks. For critical decisions about your mental health or grief journey, consult a licensed grief counselor or mental health professional. All calculations occur in your browser—no data is stored or transmitted to any server.

faq.q: What are the 5 stages of grief?
faq.a: Denial, anger, bargaining, depression, and acceptance. Developed by Elisabeth Kübler-Ross, these stages describe common emotional responses to loss, though they don't always occur in this order.
No. Stages often overlap, occur out of sequence, or cycle back. Grief is an individual process that varies widely from person to person.

faq.q: How long does grief typically last?
faq.a: Most people experience acute grief for 6-12 months after loss. Adjustment continues beyond this period, but intensity usually decreases over time.

faq.q: What support is available for grief?
faq.a: Options include grief counseling, support groups, family support, and therapy. Many communities offer free or low-cost grief services and hotlines.
Children grieve differently depending on age and developmental stage. Explain loss in age-appropriate terms, maintain routines, and provide consistent support.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes. All our calculators are completely free and require no registration or sign-up.
These results are orientative, based on established psychological models. For concerns about your mental health, consult a licensed professional.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never transmit or store your personal information on any server.
A grief framework developed by psychiatrist Elisabeth Kübler-Ross describing five stages people commonly experience after loss. It's widely used in psychology, counseling, and palliative care.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
